2604.11565.title: Phonological distances for linguistic typology and the origin of Indo-European languages
2604.11565.paper_summary: We show that short-range phoneme dependencies encode large-scale patterns of linguistic relatedness, with direct implications for quantitative typology and evolutionary linguistics. Specifically, using an information-theoretic framework, we argue that phoneme sequences modeled as second-order Markov chains essentially capture the statistical correlations of a phonological system. This finding enables us to quantify distances among 67 modern languages from a multilingual parallel corpus employing a distance metric that incorporates articulatory features of phonemes. The resulting phonological distance matrix recovers major language families and reveals signatures of contact-induced convergence. Remarkably, we obtain a clear correlation with geographic distance, allowing us to constrain a plausible homeland region for the Indo-European family, consistent with the Steppe hypothesis.
2604.11565.paper_summary_zh: 我們展示了短距離音素依賴關係編碼了語言相關性的廣泛模式，對定量類型學和演化語言學有直接的影響。<lb/>具體而言，使用信息理論框架，我們主張將音素序列建模為二階馬爾可夫鏈本質上捕捉了音韻系統的統計相關性。<lb/>這一發現使我們能夠量化來自多語言平行語料庫的67種現代語言之間的距離，並採用一種距離度量，該度量納入了音素的發音特徵。<lb/>最終得到的音韻距離矩陣恢復了主要語言家族，並揭示了接觸誘導的趨同特徵。<lb/>值得注意的是，我們獲得了與地理距離的明確相關性，這使我們能夠限制印歐語系的可能故鄉區域，與草原假說一致。
2604.11565.author: Marius Mavridis et.al.
2604.11565.authors: Marius Mavridis, Juan De Gregorio, Raul Toral, David Sanchez
